methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

private.name: Inertia Enterprises
private.rationale: Same sector (Energy & Climate), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Stardust Solutions
private.rationale: Same sector (Energy & Climate), same stage (Series A)

private.name: DG Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Energy & Climate), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Magrathea Metals is a Series A-stage energy and climate technology company founded by Alex Grant and Caleb Boyd. The company has raised $100M+ to date. The comp universe for this analysis consists of three private peers in the energy and climate sector—Inertia Enterprises, Stardust Solutions, and DG Matrix—with no publicly traded direct comparables available.

Stage-progression methodology was applied to triangulate valuation. Using the $100M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the next funding stage, this method implies a valuation of $200M. This single applicable approach anchors the analysis given limited comp density and the early-stage maturity of the frontier-energy sector.

The triangulated valuation range spans $170M (low) to $230M (high), with a median of $200M. Key uncertainties include reliance on a single valuation methodology due to sparse public market data and the absence of direct revenue or profitability metrics to validate the stage-progression assumption. The wide band between low and high estimates reflects inherent uncertainty in early-stage tech valuation when comparable trading multiples are unavailable. Additional data on traction metrics, capital efficiency, or detailed peer transaction pricing would narrow this range materially.
